exampleSentences: Anh từng sống ở Hà Nội trước kia nhưng bây giờ anh ở Sài Gòn.
Trước kia, em không biết nấu ăn nhưng bây giờ em nấu rất ngon.
Chị ấy làm việc tại ngân hàng trước kia, giờ chị ấy đã chuyển công ty.
notes:
Trước kia means “in the past” or “before now.” It talks about a previous period of your life or a situation that is no longer true.

It often contrasts with bây giờ (now), even if “bây giờ” isn’t said. You can put it at the beginning or end of a sentence.

Close synonym: ngày xưa (more nostalgic, often for childhood or long ago). It’s neutral in tone and works in everyday speech.

definitions.en: A phrase used to refer to a time in the past.
definitions.es: Una frase utilizada para referirse a un tiempo en el pasado.
definitions.vi: Cụm từ dùng để chỉ một thời điểm trong quá khứ.

example.en: In the past, I lived in Hanoi.
example.es: En el pasado, vivía en Hanói.
example.vi: Trước kia, tôi đã sống ở Hà Nội.

translation.en: Before eating, I spent time at home.

content.sentence: Trước khi ăn, tôi đã ___ thời gian ở nhà.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He used to live in Hanoi in the past but now he is in Saigon.
In the past, I didn't know how to cook but now I cook very well.
She worked at the bank in the past, now she has changed companies.
notes_md:
**Trước kia** means “in the past” or “before now.” It talks about a previous period of your life or a situation that is no longer true.

It often contrasts with **bây giờ** (now), even if “bây giờ” isn’t said. You can put it at the beginning or end of a sentence.


Close synonym: **ngày xưa** (more nostalgic, often for childhood or long ago). It’s neutral in tone and works in everyday speech.

content.sentence: Tôi đã sống ở đây ___ rất lâu.

translation.en: I lived here a long time ago.
